content: Just as human learners in high English language classrooms use the lexical concept of reflexion (reflecting on written work to spot logical gaps and improve expression) to incrementally refine their output, large language models can adopt this classroom-style iterative reflexion framework to turn scattered self-reflection across multiple perspectives into structured, progressive improvement of generation quality. This framework frames LLM multi-perspective self-reflection not as a specialized technical module, but as a teachable metacognitive habit patterned after how humans learn to use reflexion for skill growth.
